shortAnswer: Protein in urine (proteinuria) can indicate kidney damage, as healthy kidneys normally retain protein. Small amounts may appear after exercise or fever. Persistent values should be discussed with your doctor, as they may point to kidney disease.
metaDescription: Protein in urine (proteinuria)? Causes, kidney disease risk, and what the finding means — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does the proteinuria indicate kidney damage?
Should a 24-hour urine collection be done?
What measures will protect my kidney function?
pageTitle: Protein in urine — Proteinuria explained | Medyra
